DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT QORUM, INC., ANDREW PIETRA'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

Cross-defendants Qorum Inc. and Andrew Pietra's demurrer to all eight casues of action in the cross-complaint filed by QaProsoft, LLC and Solvd, Inc. is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract alleged by Solvd, sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for violation of Penal Code 496, and overruled as to all other causes of action. The breach of contract claim alleged by Solvd, a non-party to the contract, lacks ultimate facts to support Solvd's ability to sue as a successor. There is no allegation why Solvd is a successor to QaProsoft. The second through fourth causes of action for common counts are adequately plead. Cross-Complainants are entitled to plead facts or legal theories in the alternative, or even inconsistently. (Radar Co. v. Stone (1986) 178 Cal.App.3d 10, 29.) The fifth cause of action for violation of the Invasion of Privacy Act is also sufficient. (Kight v. CashCall, Inc. (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1377, 1391 ["[T]he statute prevents a corporation and an individual from secretly monitoring a conversation, regardless whether the individual is acting for his or her own purposes or on behalf of the corporation."]) The Penal Code cause of action is barred by Lacagnina v. Comprehend Systems, Inc. (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 955. The seventh and eighth causes of action for false promise and violation of the Unfair Competition Law are adequately alleged.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS QAPROSOFT, LLC, SOLVD, INC., IGOR LYSENKO, IGOR VAYNER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as follows:

The 12 demurrers filed by defendants QaProsoft, LLC; Solvd, Inc.; Igor Lysenko and Igor Vayner are all overruled:
1: A "plaintiff may plead the legal effect of the contract rather than its precise language." (Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 199.) Moreover, defendants claim no lack of clarity regarding the contract. (Id.)
2: Alter ego is adequately pled against the individual defendants. (Cmplt. 2:19-3:19.)
3: Promissory fraud is adequately pled; e.g., the complaint alleges that defendants promised Lysenko "would perform backend software development services," but "did not intend to perform" and "did not perform" on the promise; i.e., it was never intended that Lysenko would do the promised work. (Cmplt. 7:10-28.)
4: Intentional fraud is adequately pled; the complaint alleges that defendants' monthly invoices contained knowingly false entries. (Cmplt. 8:4-22.)
5&6: While a plaintiff may not recover in tort for mere contract breaches, plaintiff has adequately pled well-recognized tort theories.
7: It is alleged that defendants intended to disrupt plaintiff's contracts with third-parties. (Cmplt. 8:23-9:75.) This suffices at the pleading stage.
8: The elements of intentional and negligent interference with prospective economic advantage are pled. (CACI 2202, 2204; Cmplt. 9:11-10:16.)
9&10: Contract and tort theories - which often measure damages differently - may be pled together or in the alternative.
11: Whether civil conspiracy is a cause of action or a doctrine, it is adequately pled. (Cmplt. 10:17-11:11.) The individual defendants may be liable for conspiracy. (Doctor's Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 49 Cal.3d 39, 47.)
12: A Business & Professions Code 17200 violation need not be pled with specificity. (Quelimane Co. v. Stewart Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 46-47.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-02-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS QAPROSOFT, LLC, SOLVD, INC., IGOR LYSENKO, IGOR VAYNER's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendants recycle their demurrer arguments in arguing that the complaint's punitive damages prayers should be stricken. The order overruling the demurrers is thus incorporated by reference. At the pleading stage, plaintiff's punitive damage allegations suffice.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
